dispatch.summary: Claude reviewed task 074 implementation and found the dirty-region API technically sound: replay overhead 1.47% under the 5% budget, idle take_dirty_regions far under 2ms/frame, independent tracking-disabled versus tracking-enabled raster parity at 0 changed pixels, live 80x24/120x40/200x60 colored screenshots present, focused dirty-region unit tests present, and make check previously passing. The review correctly blocked final Done on process and evidence gates: all new scripts/artifacts must be staged, SOLID-QA must be rerun to PASS, the observation-only pixel scope must be documented, and task/progress status must match evidence.

Gemini timed out and spawned a recursive process tree with MCP children during review. It is excluded from shux review automation until explicitly requested; future second-agent review should use bounded agy once it responds reliably, or DootSabha native agy support when available.

agy is installed at /Users/indrasvat/.local/bin/agy and exposes --print plus --print-timeout, but a trivial guarded smoke prompt timed out after its print timeout. It was therefore not used as a task-074 gate in this run.

resolved_blockers_from_review: new scripts and QA artifacts are present in git status and must be committed with the feature
task documentation now states dirty regions are observation-only in task 074 and renderer consumption is out of scope
live shux evidence now includes 80x24, 120x40, and 200x60 screenshots
live screenshots include truecolor, indexed-color, and basic-color probes with saturated colored pixels
external reviewer process hygiene is now guarded by .shux/scripts/agent_review_guard.sh
remaining_gate: shux-vt-solid-qa must be rerun and write VERDICT: PASS before final completion.
